text.title: shadcn-ui-architecture Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for shadcn-ui-architecture as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Editing components/ui/button.tsx directly to "fix" a behavior instead of composing a feature component on top.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Modifying vendored primitives breaks shadcn diff upgrades and makes the ownership contract meaningless. Install CODEOWNERS or a CI guard to prevent writes to components/ui/.

failure-mode.description: Hardcoding color hex values (e.g., text-[#3b82f6]) in feature components.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hard-coded colors bypass the CSS variable theme; brand changes require a grep across every feature instead of a single globals.css update.

failure-mode.description: Omitting "use client" on Radix-based primitives (Dialog, Popover, DropdownMenu) in Next.js App Router.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Works in development (no SSR), fails silently in production server build with a cryptic hydration mismatch or undefined context error.

failure-mode.description: Path alias drift: tsconfig paths, vite.config resolve.alias, and components.json pointing to different locations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Consumers get "cannot find module @/components/ui" in one build tool but not another; the root cause is hidden by different config files not staying in sync.

failure-mode.description: Importing directly across feature boundaries (components/billing/ importing from components/dashboard/).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cross-feature imports create invisible coupling; a change to one feature silently breaks another. Enforce with dependency-cruiser or eslint-plugin-boundaries.
